topic.name: Oil and Gas Regulation
topic.description: Legislation governing royalties and environmental protections related to oil and gas extraction on the Outer Continental Shelf.

Document 1: Amends the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act to declare that any royalty under an oil and gas lease on submerged lands shall apply to all oil removed under the lease.
Increases the minimum royalty under such leases from 12.5% to 20%.

topic.name: Taxation and Taxpayer Rights
topic.description: The documents discuss various aspects of tax laws and taxpayer rights within the framework of the Internal Revenue Code.

Document 1: Taxpayer Bill of Rights Act of 2015
require the Internal Revenue Service (IRS) to ensure that IRS employees are familiar with and act in accord with taxpayer rights
Document 2: Property Tax Relief Act of 2009
allow state and local real property taxes paid on a principal residence as a deduction from gross income
Document 3: suspend the dollar limitation and other limitations on the expensing allowance
for depreciable business assets placed in service on or after September 8, 2010

topic.name: Education Policy and Funding
topic.description: The documents discuss various aspects of education policy, funding allocations, and accountability measures under federal education laws.

Document 1: holds a state, and its local educational agencies (LEAs) and schools, harmless from sanctions under the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965
specifies criteria for state assessments
Document 2: amends part A of title I of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965
establishes formulae for determining the minimum allotment to American Samoa, Guam, the Northern Mariana Islands, and the U.S. Virgin Islands of school improvement funds
Document 3: amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965
requires lenders to provide student loan applicants with certain written information regarding loan conditions

topic.name: Medicare Policy Reforms
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative reforms and amendments related to Medicare and Medicaid programs, focusing on drug pricing, eligibility, and fraud prevention.

Document 1: Amends part D (Voluntary Prescription Drug Benefit Program) of title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act (SSA) to require drug manufacturers to pay the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) drug rebates for rebate eligible (low-income) individuals.
Excludes from Medicare coverage as a part D drug any drug or biological manufactured by a manufacturer that has not entered into and have in effect a rebate agreement with the Secretary.
Document 2: Allows any individual who is otherwise entitled to benefits under part A (Hospital Insurance) of title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act (SSA) to elect to opt out of such entitlement.
Amends the Internal Revenue Code to make individuals who opt out of Medicare part A eligible for health savings accounts.
Document 3: Establish specified enhanced program and provider protections against fraud under the Medicare, Medicaid, and Children's Health Insurance programs under SSA titles XVIII, XIX, and XXI.

topic.name: Tariff Schedule Amendments
topic.description: The documents discuss temporary suspensions of duties on specific items in the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States.

Document 1: suspend temporarily the duty on certain yard ornaments depicting school mascots.
Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 2: suspend temporarily the duty on chromate(4-), ... tetrasodium
Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 3: suspend temporarily the duty on certain fiberglass sheets used to make ceiling tiles.
Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States

topic.name: Family and Medical Leave Policies
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to family and medical leave entitlements for employees, particularly focusing on caregiving for military family members and other family-related needs.

Document 1: entitle an eligible employee to up to 26 workweeks of leave
to care for a covered service member who is the employee's spouse, son, daughter, or parent.
Document 2: Amends the Family and Medical Leave Act of 1993
to extend coverage to employees at worksites where the employer employs at least 25 employees
Document 3: Entitles an employed family member of a member of the Armed Forces
to two workweeks of leave per year for each family member who is so called or deployed.

topic.name: Child Protection and Trafficking Prevention
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at protecting children from sexual exploitation, trafficking, and improving their welfare in the context of foster care.

Document 1: Prohibits providing a waiver for commissioning or enlistment in the Armed Forces for any individual convicted under federal or state law of the felony offense of rape, sexual abuse, sexual assault, incest, or any other sexual offense.
Document 2: Amends the Missing Children's Assistance Act to require the Office of Juvenile Justice and Delinquency Prevention's annual grant to the National Center for Missing and Exploited Children to be used to operate a cyber tipline to provide online users and electronic service providers an effective means of reporting Internet-related child sexual exploitation in the area of child sex trafficking.
Document 3: Amends part E (Foster Care and Adoption Assistance) of title IV of the Social Security Act to require the state plan for foster care and adoption assistance to demonstrate that the state has developed policies and procedures for identifying and screening, and for determining appropriate state action and services with respect to, children over whom the state agency has responsibility for placement, care, or supervision who the state has reasonable cause to believe are victims of sex trafficking or a severe form of trafficking in persons.

topic.name: Mortgage Regulation and Consumer Protection
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing oversight and protections in the mortgage industry to prevent fraud and ensure fair practices.

Document 1: Amends federal criminal law to make it unlawful for any mortgage professional to defraud any natural person or financial institution
Grants whistleblower protection to personnel of a widely accepted private certification board.
Document 2: Amends the Financial Stability Act of 2010 with respect to minimum leverage capital requirements and minimum risk-based capital requirements for depository institution holding companies.
Document 3: Prohibit each mortgagee (or servicer) from imposing any interest charge on an insured mortgage as a result of the loss of any time period provided by the mortgagee.

topic.name: Land Management and Acquisition
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the acquisition, management, and protection of land, particularly in relation to tribal and national forest interests.

Document 1: Authorizes the Secretary of the Interior to acquire up to 18 acres of land and interests in Mariposa, California
Requires acquired lands and interests to be administered as part of Yosemite National Park.
Document 2: This bill amends the Tribal Forest Protection Act of 2004
to establish a timeframe for consideration of tribal requests to protect Indian forest land or rangeland.
Document 3: Requires the conveyance of specified National Forest System land in the Ouachita National Forest
Makes the deposited amount available for expenditure for the acquisition of land and interests in the Ouachita National Forest.

topic.name: Political Transparency Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses legislative measures aimed at enhancing transparency in political campaign financing and communications.

Document 1: amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to revise the requirement of a 48-hour notification of a campaign contribution
requirement shall now apply to any political committee, not just a candidate's principal campaign committee
Document 2: require any candidate of a major party for the office of President to file with the Federal Election Commission (FEC) a copy of the candidate's income tax returns
shall be made publicly available at the same time and in the same manner as other reports and statements
Document 3: directs the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) to revise the do-not-call registry provisions
prohibit politically-oriented recorded message telephone calls to telephone numbers listed on that registry

topic.name: Immigration Employment Regulations
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation related to employment regulations for non-citizens and the accountability of employers in hiring practices.

Document 1: requires a federal contractor who employs directly or through a subcontractor an alien who is not authorized to work to be debarred or suspended from federal contracting
prohibits the Department of Homeland Security from contracting with a company that employs individuals unless the company agrees to participate in the basic pilot employment confirmation program
Document 2: amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to increase employer civil fines for hiring an alien that the employer knows is not authorized for employment
sets forth a federal-state allocation of such additional penalty funds
Document 3: amends citizenship and residency qualifications for enlistment in the armed forces to permit enlistment of additional persons who have resided continuously in a lawful status in the United States
requires authorized enlistees who are not citizens to be adjusted to the status of an alien lawfully admitted for permanent residence

topic.name: Military Policy and Oversight
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation and policies related to the Department of Defense and its oversight, including data collection on suicide rates, land entitlement definitions, and accountability within the intelligence community.

Document 1: Requires the Secretary of Defense (DOD) to submit to Congress a policy for the development of a standard method for collecting, reporting, and assessing suicide data
involving members of the Armed Forces, including reserve components, and their dependents.
Document 2: Revises the definition of 'entitlement land' for which the Secretary of the Interior is required to make payments to local governments
to include federal land under the jurisdiction of the Secretary of Defense (DOD) or the Secretary of a military department.
Document 3: Amends the National Security Act of 1947 to require the Director of National Intelligence to report annually to the congressional intelligence committees
on violations of law or executive order by personnel of an element of the intelligence community.

topic.name: Food Labeling and Safety Regulations
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at enhancing food labeling transparency and ensuring safety standards regarding harmful substances in food products.

Document 1: Trans Fat Truth in Labeling Act of 2013
require the label of a food that has less than 0.5 grams and greater than 0.0 grams of trans fat per serving
Document 2: Food Ingredient Right to Know Act
deem a food misbranded if it contains any spice, flavoring, or coloring derived from meat, poultry, any other animal product
Document 3: Arsenic Prevention and Protection from Lead Exposure in Juice Act of 2012
limit the quantity of total arsenic and lead in beverages containing fruit juice

topic.name: Mental Health Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on enhancing mental health services and support systems in various contexts, including emergencies and for specific populations like exonerees and veterans.

Document 1: establish the National Center for Public Mental Health Emergency Preparedness
coordinate and implement the development and delivery of mental health services in the event of bioterrorism or other public health emergency
Document 2: provide support services (e.g., employment training, health care services, and legal assistance) to exonerees
has been determined to be factually innocent of the crime
Document 3: transitioning from receiving treatment from the Department of Defense to receiving treatment from the Department of Veterans Affairs
receive the same level of priority as members of the Armed Forces at the facility

topic.name: Rural and Agricultural Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative amendments related to rural housing, worker eligibility in nutrition assistance, and motor carrier regulations for agricultural transport.

Document 1: Amends the Housing Act of 1949 to revise the requirements for consideration as rural areas
has a serious lack of mortgage credit for lower and moderate-income families.
Document 2: eliminate the provision making a federal, state, or local government employee who is dismissed for participating in a strike
ineligible for the supplemental nutrition assistance program.
Document 3: Amends the Motor Carrier Safety Improvement Act of 1999
extends the exemptions to drivers transporting agricultural farm supplies.

topic.name: U.S. Sanctions and Hostage Policy
topic.description: This topic encompasses U.S. legislative measures aimed at imposing sanctions on Iran and Syria, as well as addressing hostage situations involving U.S. citizens in Iran.

Document 1: Iran Financial System Access Limitation Act of 2016
The President may not issue a license to an offshore dollar-clearing entity to conduct a transaction in U.S. dollars with an Iranian financial institution.
Document 2: Iran is unjustly holding at least three U.S. citizens
The President is authorized to take necessary action to detain non-diplomatic officials of the government of Iran.
Document 3: U.S. sanctions, controls, and regulations relating to Syria shall remain in effect
Imposes specified trade, assistance, and military sanctions, as appropriate, on persons or countries that transfer goods or technology.

topic.name: Veterans and Social Security Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to the protection and distribution of benefits for veterans and adjustments to social security laws regarding marriage and retirement income disclosures.

Document 1: Excludes monetary veterans' benefits paid to a veteran from a state or municipality from consideration as income for purposes of pension benefits paid by the Secretary of Veterans Affairs (VA).
Document 2: Amends title II of the Social Security Act with respect to the determination of a valid marriage to account for marriages in jurisdictions other than a state.
Document 3: Amends the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 to require the quarterly pension benefit statement to include a lifetime income disclosure.

topic.name: Transportation Policy and Regulations
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures and regulations related to transportation safety, passenger rights, and the management of transportation services.

Document 1: Pets on Trains Act of 2013 - Directs the National Railroad Passenger Corporation (Amtrak) to propose a pet policy
Requires Amtrak to collect a fee for each cat or dog transported by a ticketed passenger
Document 2: Amends the Safe, Accountable, Flexible, Efficient Transportation Equity Act: A Legacy for Users
authorization of appropriations funded by the Highway Trust Fund for National Highway Traffic Safety Administration highway safety programs
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Transportation to complete a rulemaking that prohibits air carriers from charging fees for carry-on baggage
requires air carriers to notify air passengers of such baggage restrictions before they arrive at the airport

topic.name: Fishery Conservation Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the conservation and management of fishery resources and ecosystems in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Magnuson-Stevens Fishery Conservation and Management Act to add New York State as a member of the New England Fishery Management Council.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Commerce to conduct a benchmark stock assessment of the Gulf of Mexico red snapper fishery and report to Congress.
Prohibits the Secretary from promulgating any interim rule under specified provisions of the Magnuson-Stevens Fishery Conservation and Management Act.
Document 3: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to extend the award of remaining coral reef conservation program grant funds to appropriate projects.
Deems specified terms to include any coral reef that is subject to the jurisdiction of the United States.

topic.name: Labor Rights Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents addresses various legislative measures aimed at protecting labor rights and ensuring fair practices in employer-employee relationships.

Document 1: Amends the National Labor Relations Act to make it an unfair labor practice for an employer to recognize or bargain collectively with a labor organization that has not been selected by a majority of the employees in a secret ballot election.
Prohibits a labor organization from causing or attempting to cause an employer to recognize or bargain collectively with a representative that has not been selected in such manner.
Document 2: Prohibits an employer from discharging or discriminating against any employee that disseminates or assists in the dissemination of any message on the employer's property.
Limits this prohibition to any employer whose annual gross revenue exceeds $5 million.
Document 3: Directs federal agencies to ensure that bid specifications do not require or prohibit a bidder from entering into agreements with a labor organization.
Allows exemptions to avert an imminent threat to public health or safety or to serve national security.

topic.name: Communications Regulation and Licensing
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the regulation, licensing, and operational requirements of communication services and technologies in the United States.

Document 1: Requires a satellite carrier whose secondary transmissions are subject to statutory licensing to deposit a filing fee semiannually with the Register of Copyrights.
Applies statutory licensing provisions to systems of three or more noncommercial educational broadcast stations.
Document 2: Amends the Digital Television Transition and Public Safety Act of 2005 to extend through FY2012 the requirement that the Assistant Secretary for Communications and Information make payments from the Digital Television Transition and Public Safety Fund.
Grant program to assist public safety agencies in the acquisition of interoperable communications equipment.
Document 3: Requires the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to promulgate regulations requiring a commercial mobile service provider to notify a subscriber who has used in excess of a certain percentage of the monthly limit.
Ensure that any notice of possible excess usage, and the consent to use excess services, are not charged to the subscriber's account.

topic.name: Cybersecurity and Privacy Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at enhancing cybersecurity practices and protecting personal information in various sectors.

Document 1: Directs the federal government to conduct cybersecurity activities to provide shared situational awareness
facilitate information sharing, interaction, and collaboration among and between federal, state, local, tribal, and territorial governments
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services to notify individuals within one hour after the discovery of a breach
requires the Secretary to report to Congress annually on all such breaches that occurred within the past year
Document 3: Directs the Federal Trade Commission to initiate a rulemaking to require covered entities to carry out security measures
Defines 'covered entity' as a person who collects, uses, transfers, or stores such information concerning more than 5,000 individuals

topic.name: Wildlife Conservation Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at the protection and management of wildlife species and their habitats in the context of military activities and hunting regulations.

Document 1: authorizes payments to conservation banking programs
military readiness activities impacting protected species
Document 2: amends the Marine Mammal Protection Act
allows importation of polar bear parts from sport hunts
Document 3: amends the Endangered Species Act
prohibits treatment of the Gray Wolf as endangered

topic.name: Small Business Support Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on providing financial and educational support to small businesses in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow certain small businesses to defer payment of income tax
establish tax-exempt GROW Accounts to provide financing and tax relief for such small businesses.
Document 2: directs the Small Business Administration to develop partnership agreements that arrange for development of high-quality training for small businesses
training in person or through a website relating to domestic and international intellectual property protections.
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow a new business-related tax credit for 30% of the equity investment in a small business concern

Requires the Secretary of the Treasury to provide to the Administrator of the Small Business Administration any data on the implementation and use of the equity investment in small business tax credit.

topic.name: U.S. Postal Service Designations
topic.description: Documents related to the designation of U.S. Postal Service facilities in honor of individuals and boundary adjustments of national parks.

Document 1: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 1400 Highway 41 North in Inverness, Florida, as the 'Chief Warrant Officer Aaron Weaver Post Office Building.'
Document 2: The Department of the Interior may acquire land or interests, by donation or exchange, from willing owners only.
Document 3: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 523 East Railroad Street in Knox, Pennsylvania, as the 'Specialist Ross A. McGinnis Memorial Post Office.'

topic.name: Election and Campaign Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at regulating election practices, voter protection, and campaign financing.

Document 1: Amends the federal criminal code to make it unlawful for anyone before or during a federal election to knowingly communicate, or attempt to communicate, false election-related information
Increases from one year to five years' imprisonment the criminal penalty for intimidation of voters.
Document 2: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to repeal the limitation on party expenditures on behalf of candidates in general elections.

topic.name: U.S.-Cuba Legislative Measures
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative actions and bills related to the United States' policies and regulations concerning Cuba.

Document 1: prohibits the President from modifying, terminating, abandoning, or transferring the lease with the government of Cuba
Congress enacts a law authorizing the change
Document 2: prohibits the use of funds made available to the Department of the Treasury to implement, administer, or enforce regulations to require specific licenses for travel-related transactions directly related to educational activities in Cuba
Document 3: amends the Tariff Act of 1930 to revise eligibility requirements for the drawback of duties on merchandise exported from the United States

topic.name: Native Hawaiian Housing Assistance
topic.description: Legislation and initiatives aimed at providing housing assistance and loan guarantees for Native Hawaiians.

Document 1: Amends the Native American Housing Assistance and Self-Determination Act of 1996
authorization of appropriations for housing assistance for Native Hawaiians
Document 2: ensure that the flags of the states, District of Columbia, American Samoa, Guam, Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands, U.S. Virgin Islands, and Puerto Rico, encircle the Washington Monument
Document 3: Amends the Native American Housing Assistance and Self-Determination Act of 1996
make the Department of Hawaiian Home Lands of the state of Hawaii eligible for loan guarantees

topic.name: Minor Abortion Regulations
topic.description: This topic encompasses federal laws aimed at regulating the transportation and parental involvement in minors' abortion decisions.

Document 1: prohibit transporting a minor child across a state line to obtain an abortion
requires parental involvement in the minor's abortion decision
Document 2: prohibit knowingly transporting a minor child across a state line to obtain an abortion
requires parental consent or notification or judicial authorization
Document 3: imposes criminal penalties on anyone who knowingly performs an abortion based on sex, gender, color or race
transport a woman into the United States or across a state line for the purpose of obtaining such an abortion

topic.name: Postal and Food Security Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on improving postal service governance and addressing childhood hunger through federal initiatives.

Document 1: procedures governing the closure or consolidation of postal branches
applicable to post offices
Document 2: amends the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act
promote the reduction or elimination of childhood hunger

Document 3: removes the authority of the U.S. Postal Service to issue semipostal stamps

topic.name: Mining and Environmental Remediation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the remediation of abandoned mining sites and the management of mining claims and townsites.

Document 1: provisions to encourage the partial or complete remediation of inactive and abandoned mine sites
permits from requirements of the National Environmental Policy Act
Document 2: expedited program to examine and determine the validity of each unpatented mining claim
convey to the appropriate county, without consideration, all right, title, and interest of the United States in and to any such mining townsites

topic.name: Commemorative Legislation and National Identity
topic.description: This cluster addresses U.S. legislative efforts to honor and preserve national heritage through memorials and displays that reflect historical significance and cultural values.

Document 1: Requires the Architect of the Capitol (AOC) to ensure that the Pledge of Allegiance to the Flag and the national motto 'In God We Trust' are each displayed prominently in the Capitol Visitor Center.
Prohibits the AOC from removing or refusing to include any language, symbol, writing, document, or record on the grounds that they include a religious reference or Judeo-Christian content.
Document 2: This bill directs the Department of the Army to place in Arlington National Cemetery a memorial honoring helicopter pilots and crew members who served on active duty in the Armed Forces during the Vietnam era.
Document 3: Authorizes the National Capital Planning Commission to modify the boundaries of Areas I and II of the National Mall reserved for the location of commemorative works.
Requires the Commission to prepare recommendations for improving upon the process required for the selection, design, and subject matter of commemorative works in the District of Columbia.

topic.name: Regulatory Framework for Safety Compliance
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at enhancing safety compliance and accountability across various transportation sectors, including unmanned aircraft systems, motor vehicles, and gas pipelines.

Document 1: amends the FAA Modernization and Reform Act to permit a person to operate a small commercial unmanned aircraft
the owner must register the drone; the operator must pass a test
Document 2: Revises civil penalties for violation of federal motor vehicle safety requirements
Increases civil penalties for violations: from $5,000 to $25,000 per violation
Document 3: requires each operator of a gas pipeline facility to accelerate the repair, rehabilitation, and replacement of gas piping
develop prioritized timelines to repair or replace all leaking or high-risk piping or equipment

topic.name: Military and Compensation Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on military compensation, appropriations, and corporate tax reforms related to employee compensation.

Document 3: Makes supplemental appropriations for FY2008 to the Department of Agriculture for the Foreign Agricultural Service for grants under the Agricultural Trade Development and Assistance Act of 1954.

topic.name: Legislative Reforms for Equality and Rights
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts aimed at enhancing rights and protections for individuals, particularly in the context of jury service, bankruptcy, and water rights management.

Document 1: prohibit the exclusion of citizens from service as a grand or petit juror
expanding the current prohibition on exclusion on account of race, color, religion, sex, national origin, or economic status
Document 2: declare nondischargeable in bankruptcy any debts for a personal injury that results in a permanent disability
Document 3: authorize the Redwood Valley County Water District to enter into additional non-federal obligations
requires the Secretary to reschedule the payments due under specified loans

topic.name: Immigration and Family Policy
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at reforming immigration processes and enhancing protections for families and workers within the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to include permanent partners
within the scope of such Act.
Document 2: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to authorize the Secretary of Labor to enforce federal labor laws
prohibits H-2B worker entry until the Secretary certifies that prevailing wages will be paid.
Document 3: Establishes within the Department of State the Office for Orphan Policy Diplomacy and Development
supports the preservation and reunification of families.

topic.name: Government Training and Safety Policies
topic.description: This topic encompasses government mandates for training and safety protocols in various sectors, including defense, education, and firefighting.

Document 1: training in the management of defense contracts and contractors
additional training for members with direct responsibility for decisions regarding contracting
Document 2: develop and make publicly available a written school safety policy
guidance to such LEAs by taking the steps necessary
Document 3: establish an interagency working group to study the use of unmanned aircraft systems for wildland firefighting
develop standards and best practices for the use of unmanned aircraft systems

topic.name: Greenhouse Gas Emissions Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures and programs aimed at regulating and reducing greenhouse gas emissions through market-driven approaches and incentives.

Document 1: establish and maintain the National Greenhouse Gas Database
program for market-driven reduction of GHGs through the use of tradeable allowances
Document 2: Prohibits federal agencies from imposing a fee or tax on gaseous emissions emitted directly by livestock
Document 3: establish a carbon incentives program to achieve supplemental greenhouse gas emissions reductions
provide financial incentive payments for activities that increase carbon sequestration and storage

topic.name: Regulatory Oversight and Advisory Committees
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at improving regulatory processes and the functioning of federal advisory committees to enhance governance and accountability.

Document 1: Establishes in the legislative branch the Regulatory Improvement Commission.
for the purpose of reducing compliance costs, encouraging growth and innovation, improving competitiveness, and protecting public health and safety.
Document 2: Amends the Federal Advisory Committee Act (FACA) to require appointments to federal agency advisory committees to be made without regard to political affiliation or activity.

Requires each agency head to ensure that advice and recommendations of an advisory committee that reports to the agency are the result of the advisory committee's judgment, independent from the agency.

topic.name: Government Security and Oversight
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at enhancing government accountability, national security, and the prevention of terrorism-related activities.

Document 1: Establishes the Independent Government Waste Reduction Board
advises specific implementation of the recommendations from the March 2011 Government Accountability Office (GAO) report
Document 2: establish: (1) the Committee on Hostage Recovery within the National Security Council (NSC)
coordinating retrieval of U.S. hostages among federal departments and agencies
Document 3: grant the Attorney General the authority to deny the transfer of firearms
determines that such individual has been engaged in or has supported terrorist activities

topic.name: Health Policy and Assistance
topic.description: This cluster focuses on U.S. legislation aimed at improving health care services and support for vulnerable populations, including those affected by HIV/AIDS and Pacific Islanders, as well as international assistance for recovery efforts in Haiti.

Document 1: Amends provisions of title XXVI of the Public Health Service Act to extend the RWCA
Grants for HIV/AIDS education and training for health care personnel
Document 2: Direct the Secretary of Health and Human Services to develop and implement an ongoing and sustainable national strategy for identifying and evaluating the health status and health care needs of NHOPI populations
Conduct a preliminary health survey to identify the major regions of such areas in which NHOPI people reside
Document 3: Support the sustainable recovery and rebuilding of Haiti
Promote development and rebuilding led by all levels of Haitian government

topic.name: Health Care Antitrust Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at promoting competition and preventing anticompetitive practices in the health insurance and pharmaceutical industries.

Document 1: Prohibits the McCarran-Ferguson Act from being construed to permit health insurance or medical malpractice insurance issuers to engage in price fixing, bid rigging, or market allocations.
Applies prohibitions of unfair methods of competition to the business of health insurance without regard to whether the business is for profit.
Document 2: Enhance competition in the pharmaceutical market by stopping anticompetitive agreements between brand name and generic drug manufacturers.
Support the purpose and intent of antitrust laws by prohibiting anticompetitive practices in the pharmaceutical industry that harm consumers.
Document 3: Eliminate the requirement that a participant in an HSA be enrolled in a high deductible health care plan.
Increase the maximum contribution amount to an HSA.

topic.name: Legislative Amendments in Public Services
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative changes aimed at improving public services, including telecommunications, education support, and disaster management.

Document 1: Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to repeal provisions relating to assistance for the planning and construction of public telecommunications facilities.
regarding grants for such facilities and a requirement to develop a related long term plan.
Document 2: Amends the Department of Education Organization Act to create an Office of Specialized Instructional Support within the Department of Education.
to establish a Reduced Barriers to Learning program authorizing the Secretary of Education to award competitive matching grants to states.

topic.name: Legislative Acts on Social Issues
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts aimed at addressing social issues such as education, substance control, and human rights.

Document 1: Amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965
allows a borrower of a loan under the William D. Ford Federal Direct Loan program to modify the interest rate
Document 2: Amends the Controlled Substances Act to provide that, in determining whether a controlled substance analogue was intended for human consumption
the marketing, advertising, and labeling of the substance
Document 3: Directs the President to develop and transmit to Congress a comprehensive strategy to end serious human rights violations in Sudan
Requires the strategy to include an interagency framework to plan, coordinate, and review the diplomatic, economic, intelligence, and military actions

topic.name: Government Transparency and Election Reform
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative efforts aimed at enhancing transparency in government operations and reforming election processes to promote fairness and accessibility.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 (FECA) with respect to eligibility and qualifying contribution requirements and benefits of fair elections financing of Senate election campaigns
establishment of a Fair Elections Fund, eligibility for Fund allocations, contribution and expenditure requirements
Document 2: Requires the Director of the Office of Management and Budget (OMB) to create a separate website about the reports issued by each Office of Inspector General
a general description of the role of the Office of Inspector General
Document 3: Amends the federal judicial code to authorize a representative of the news media to file a special motion to dismiss any claim asserted against such representative in a civil action
establishing a judicial procedure to seek dismissal of what are commonly referred to as 'Strategic Lawsuits Against Public Participation' [SLAPPs]

topic.name: U.S. Legislative Acts Overview
topic.description: A collection of U.S. legislative acts addressing various public policy issues including foreign policy, transportation, and federal security.

Document 1: U.S. policy to recognize Jerusalem as the undivided capital of the State of Israel
Requires any official U.S. document which lists countries and their capital cities to identify Jerusalem as Israel's capital.
Document 2: Authorizes the Department of Transportation (DOT) to make grants to designated recipients
to increase the use of public transportation by undergraduate or graduate students attending an institution of higher education.
Document 3: Amends the federal criminal code to revise the prohibition against entering restricted federal buildings or grounds
Defines 'restricted buildings or grounds' as a posted, cordoned off, or otherwise restricted area of the White House or its grounds.

topic.name: Legislative Amendments and Regulations
topic.description: The documents discuss various legislative amendments aimed at regulating substances, computer trespass, and budgetary procedures.

Document 1: Amends the Controlled Substances Act to authorize the Attorney General to exempt a scheduled listed chemical product from specified requirements
determining that it is not practical by processes known to be employed by clandestine laboratory operators to use the product in the illicit manufacture of methamphetamine.
Document 2: Amends the federal criminal code to exclude from the definition of 'computer trespasser' any person who has any relationship with the owner or operator of a protected computer
limit the length of warrantless surveillance of a computer trespasser to the earlier of 96 hours or as soon as communications that are sought are obtained.
Document 3: Amends procedures in the Congressional Budget Act of 1974 that make it out of order in the Senate to consider appropriations legislation until the Senate agrees to a concurrent resolution on the budget
Expands such new budgetary treatment to financial investments beginning in FY2015.

topic.name: Legislation on Animal and Environmental Protection
topic.description: This cluster discusses legislative measures aimed at protecting animals, particularly great apes, and addressing environmental and operational standards within federal agencies.

Document 1: Prohibits conducting invasive research on great apes
Defines 'invasive research' as research that may cause death, injury, pain, distress, fear, or trauma to great apes.
Document 3: authorizes appropriations for the Coast Guard's environmental compliance and restoration functions
the Coast Guard may use funds authorized for designing and constructing an icebreaker that is capable of buoy tending to enhance ice breaking capacity on the Great Lakes.

topic.name: U.S. Innovation and Environmental Policy
